text: Welcome to Dizima Architecture. My name is Andrea and I'm here with Ram from United Airlines.
Hi Ram, welcome to the show. Hi Andrea, excited to be here. So what does United Airlines do?
United as everyone knows is a global airline company with hundreds of destinations around
the world. Wonderful. So today we're here to talk about the centralized backup solution that
you built on AWS. In a nutshell, what is it? So first of all, I am part of the cloud engineering
team where we govern all the AWS accounts at United. And this solution we came up because
we wanted to protect United cloud infrastructure from a ransomware event. Okay, so it sits on
AWS and I want to dive straight into the architecture. So assume I'm an application owner, I need
to back up my applications. Can you walk us through the flow? Where does it start?
Okay, sure. Thanks Andrea. So every application has an IAM role, right? So when that IAM role
is created, that triggers, this IAM role triggers a cloud trail event. So the cloud trail event
is forwarded to the event bridge in the master account. This part is a chailed account, like
you know, one of the chailed accounts we have, more than 150 accounts. This is a central account
where we manage all this central backup solution. So this event bridge triggers a step function.
And the step function takes the cloud trail as a payload. And so the step function is a
series of lambda functions. Okay. And the lambda function that creates a backup.
I see. It creates a backup. So part of that one is like it creates a backup vault, it creates
a backup policy. And the backup policy tells the backup how often the backup needs to be
taken and where the backup needs to be copied. I see. So it's a series of events occurring,
you know, leading to creation of the backup vaults as well as the replication plan.
Exactly. Right? And how many, tell us, you know, how many backups are you performing?
So we backup into, we backup a lot. Like for example, each application has one backup vault.
But in general, we have two primary regions. So the backup, when a backup is taken, it copies
it to both the regions as well as it copies it to a network isolated region to protect it
from a ransomware event. Okay. To protect it from compromised. From compromise.
From being compromised. Yes. And you mentioned, you know, the number of child accounts that you carry.

almost upward of 175. 175. Okay. Yes. Yeah. And then how many applications are you protecting

Okay. And that number is fast increasing as we, as the AWS presence increases, uh, within United.
And we almost backup two petabytes of data. Wow. Um, so.
